summary: All-in-One Conversion Tool App website with five main modules: Image Editor, PDF Converter, Image Converter, Passport Size Photo Converter, and Image Background Remover. Features dark/light mode, mobile-responsive modern UI with smooth animations. A new Multimedia App is also being built with four modules: MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, and IP Camera.
architectural_notes: Client-side processing preferred for image/PDF operations to avoid server costs and privacy concerns
Use canvas API or libraries like Fabric.js for image editing; PDF-lib or pdf.js for PDF operations
Support drag-and-drop file input alongside gallery/camera selection
Passport Size Photo Converter uses Canvas API for image resizing, HD enhancement (sharpness/brightness/contrast filters), and A4 sheet grid layout generation for print/download

Image Background Remover uses Canvas API with brush-based mask painting for manual background selection; transparent PNG output via canvas compositing; no server-side AI required (optional client-side model)
APK conversion requires wrapping the web app in a WebView shell (e.g., using Capacitor or Cordova) or a PWA-to-APK tool (e.g., TWA/Bubblewrap)

Video Downloader tab uses YouTube Data API v3 (key: AIzaSyDlj5yvdIpE6OYJnIw_oJg5Cz5_kRJ33YQ) for YouTube video metadata/download; Facebook video download may require third-party proxy or scraping approach due to API restrictions

PDF Scanner tab uses MediaDevices.getUserMedia for live camera feed; document edge detection for auto-crop can use canvas-based algorithms or a library like OpenCV.js; brightness/contrast/darken-text adjustments applied via Canvas 2D filters before PDF assembly
Camera/getUserMedia access must be restricted to the PDF Scanner component only; all other modules (Image Editor, Background Remover, Passport Photo, etc.) must not request camera permission

Multimedia App uses client-side Web APIs: MediaRecorder/getUserMedia for IP Camera streaming, HTMLVideoElement/canvas for video trimming, Web Audio API or a library (e.g., wavesurfer.js / ffmpeg.wasm) for MP3 cutting
known_issues: HEIC format support is limited in browsers and may require a conversion library (e.g., heic2any)
Camera access requires HTTPS and user permission grants
True AI-based background removal requires a client-side ML model (e.g., ONNX/TensorFlow.js); manual brush selection is the primary fallback
APK packaging is not natively supported in a browser-only stack; requires a build tool like Capacitor, Cordova, or Bubblewrap/TWA
Direct YouTube/Facebook video downloading via browser is restricted by CORS and platform ToS; a server-side proxy or third-party service may be required for actual video download functionality
Background Remover: uploaded image does not appear in the canvas/preview after upload
PDF Scanner: camera access requires HTTPS and user permission; auto document edge detection in-browser is complex and may require OpenCV.js or a similar library loaded from CDN

IP Camera feature requires the device to act as a server; broadcasting the stream to other browsers over WiFi from a pure browser context is not natively supported — requires a WebRTC signaling server or a native app bridge
Client-side video/audio cutting with ffmpeg.wasm is feasible but has large bundle size (~30MB); consider lazy-loading

existing_features.title: Create a tabbed navigation layout with three main tabs: 'Image Editor', 'PDF Converter', and 'Image Converter'. Include a dark mode / light mode toggle in the header. The layout must be fully mobile-responsive with smooth tab-switching animations.

Implement the Image Editor module. Users can upload an image from their local device (file picker for Gallery) or capture via Camera (using browser MediaDevices API). Editing options must include: crop (free ratio and preset custom ratios), resize (custom width/height), rotate (90°/180°/270°) and flip (horizontal/vertical), and adjustments for brightness, contrast, and saturation using sliders. Implement text overlay: add text with multiple font family choices, font size slider, font color picker, text shadow toggle, text outline toggle, and ability to drag and rotate the text freely on the canvas. Add image filter presets: Vintage, Black & White, HDR, and Cinematic. Allow export/save in JPG, PNG, and WEBP formats with a high-quality export option.

Implement the PDF Converter module using a client-side PDF library (pdf-lib and pdfjs-dist). Allow users to add multiple PDF files. Features must include: merge multiple PDFs into one, split a PDF at a chosen page, remove selected pages from a PDF, rearrange pages via drag-and-drop thumbnails. Also support creating a new PDF from scratch by adding images (from file picker or camera capture), auto-enhancing images via a 'Clear Picture' button (apply brightness boost and sharpness/contrast correction using Canvas filters), compressing the PDF, and exporting the final PDF at high quality.

Implement the Image Converter module. Users can upload one or more images (batch support). Provide format conversion between JPG, PNG, and WEBP. Provide resize options: custom width and height inputs, and resize-by-percentage input. Provide resolution/DPI adjustment and an image quality percentage slider (for lossy formats). Apply filter presets (same set as Image Editor). Show a before/after preview (side-by-side or slider comparison). Export all converted images, individually or as a ZIP for batch jobs.

Apply a cohesive, modern visual theme across all three modules. Use a dark-first design with a clean, minimalist aesthetic: deep charcoal/slate backgrounds, vibrant teal/cyan accent colors, crisp white typography, subtle glassmorphism card surfaces, and smooth micro-interaction animations (button hover states, panel slide-ins, progress indicators). Enforce this design system consistently across all tabs, modals, and controls.

Add a 'Passport Photo' tab and an 'BG Remover' tab to the existing TabNavigation component, alongside the three existing tabs (Image Editor, PDF Converter, Image Converter). Update App.tsx to conditionally render the new PassportPhotoConverter and BackgroundRemover module components based on the active tab state. Maintain existing tab styling and smooth switching animations.

Implement the Passport Size Photo Converter module as a new frontend component at frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter.tsx. Users can upload a JPG, PNG, or JPEG image via file picker and see an instant preview. Automatically crop and resize the image to standard passport size (35mm × 45mm at 300 DPI = 413×531 px) using Canvas. Apply automatic enhancements on the Canvas: brightness boost, contrast correction, and sharpness filter. Provide a customizable size option (dropdown with common presets: 35×45mm, 2×2in, 1.5×1.5in, plus a custom width/height input in mm).

Within the Passport Photo Converter module, add a 'Number of Copies' selector (numeric input or stepper, range 1–40). Render the selected number of passport photo copies in a responsive grid layout on the right side of the workspace. Arrange copies to fill an A4 sheet layout (210×297mm) with proper margins and spacing between photos, previewing how they will look when printed.

Within the Passport Photo Converter module, add an 'Add Text' button that opens a text customization panel. Allow users to type custom text that appears at the bottom of each passport photo copy. Provide controls for: font family (dropdown with at least 5 common families), font size (slider or numeric input), font color (color picker), bold toggle, italic toggle, and text alignment (left, center, right). Render the text overlay on the Canvas below the photo.

Within the Passport Photo Converter module, add Download and Print buttons. The Download button exports the A4 sheet with all copies as a high-quality PNG (via Canvas toDataURL) or PDF (using pdf-lib loaded from CDN, same pattern as PDFConverter module). Provide a format selector (PNG or PDF). The Print button triggers window.print() with the A4 layout. Show a full preview of the final sheet before downloading.

Implement the Image Background Remover module as a new frontend component at frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx. Provide a 'Browse Photo' button to upload JPG, PNG, or JPEG images and show an instant Canvas-based preview. Implement a Crop feature with an adjustable crop box overlay on the Canvas (resize handles, zoom slider, and a rotate control). Apply the crop before any background removal processing.

Within the Background Remover module, implement a Brush Selection Tool. When the 'Brush Tool' button is activated, the user can paint over the background area on the Canvas to mark it for removal. Provide a brush size selector (small ~10px, medium ~25px, large ~50px) rendered as a circular cursor. Implement an Undo button that removes the last brush stroke, and an Erase mode toggle that allows the user to unpaint previously marked areas. Marked background areas should be highlighted with a semi-transparent red overlay.

Within the Background Remover module, implement the 'Remove Background' button. When clicked, process the Canvas to remove all pixels marked by the brush tool. Offer two output options: transparent background (exported as PNG) or a custom solid color background (color picker to choose the replacement color). Optionally provide an 'Auto Detect' button that uses a flood-fill or color-similarity algorithm to automatically select background pixels without manual brush painting.

Within the Background Remover module, implement a before/after preview showing the original uploaded image and the background-removed result side by side or via a draggable slider comparison (reuse the BeforeAfterPreview pattern from the ImageConverter module). Add a Download button that saves the result as PNG (transparent) or JPG (with selected background color) at full resolution. Add extra utility controls: zoom in/out buttons for the canvas view, an 'Add Background Image' option to upload a new background image that replaces the removed area, and a Reset button that clears all edits and returns to the original uploaded image.

Apply the existing dark-first glassmorphism design system (deep charcoal/slate backgrounds, vibrant teal/cyan accents, crisp white typography, glass-card surfaces, tool-btn classes, and micro-interaction animations) consistently to both the Passport Photo Converter and Background Remover modules. Reuse existing CSS utility classes (glass-card, tool-btn, upload-zone) defined in frontend/src/index.css. Ensure both modules are fully mobile-responsive.

In the Image Editor module (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor.tsx), add a prominent 'Download' button that is visible after an image has been loaded and edited. Clicking it should open the existing ExportDialog (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/ExportDialog.tsx) allowing the user to select format (JPEG, PNG, WebP) and quality, then trigger a file download of the current canvas state.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx), add a 'Next' button to each tab in the tabbed interface to guide users through a linear workflow: Files tab → Merge/Split tab → Pages tab → Create tab. On the Files tab, the Next button advances to the Merge/Split tab. On the Merge/Split tab, the Next button advances to the Pages tab. On the Pages tab, the Next button advances to the Create tab. On the Create tab, after the process is complete, show a 'Download' button to download the resulting PDF. Each Next button should be placed prominently at the bottom of the current tab panel.

In the Image Converter module (frontend/src/modules/ImageConverter.tsx), add a 'Next' button to each tab in the tabbed interface (Upload, Settings, Preview, Export) to guide users through a linear step-by-step workflow. On the Upload tab, Next advances to Settings. On the Settings tab, Next advances to Preview. On the Preview tab, Next advances to Export. On the Export tab, provide the existing export/download controls. Each Next button should appear at the bottom of its tab panel and be disabled when the prerequisite step is incomplete (e.g., no images uploaded on the Upload tab).

In the Image Editor module (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor.tsx and related files), add a 'Download as ZIP' button that becomes visible after an image has been loaded. When clicked, it should export the current edited canvas as an image file (using the currently selected or default format: JPEG/PNG/WebP) and package it into a ZIP archive using JSZip (already declared in frontend/src/types/declarations.d.ts), then trigger a browser download of the ZIP file.

In the Passport Photo Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/ExportPanel.tsx), add a 'Download as ZIP' option alongside the existing PNG/PDF download. When selected, the A4 sheet export (PNG) and/or individual passport photo copies should be packaged into a ZIP archive using JSZip and downloaded as a ZIP file.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/ExportPanel.tsx), add a 'Download as ZIP' button in the export controls. When clicked, the background-removed result image (PNG with transparent background or JPG with chosen background color) should be packaged into a ZIP archive using JSZip and downloaded.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx), add a 'Download as ZIP' button on the Create tab (alongside the existing PDF download button). When clicked, the final generated PDF should be packaged into a ZIP archive using JSZip and downloaded. If multiple PDFs have been processed (e.g., after a split operation), all resulting PDF files should be included in the ZIP.

In the Image Editor module (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor.tsx), add a prominent 'Crop' button to the main editing toolbar that is visible whenever an image is loaded. Clicking the button should activate the existing CropTool panel (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/CropTool.tsx), showing the preset aspect ratio options (Free, 1:1, 4:3, 16:9, 3:2) and allowing the user to apply the crop to the canvas. The button should follow the existing tool-btn styling used throughout the editor and highlight/indicate active state when the crop panel is open.

In the Image Editor Crop tab (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/CropTool.tsx), when the user clicks the 'Crop' button, display an interactive crop selection overlay directly on the canvas. The overlay must show a draggable, resizable crop rectangle with corner and edge handles so the user can visually define the exact area to crop. Include a visual dimming effect outside the crop region. Provide a 'Apply Crop' confirmation button and a 'Cancel' button. When Apply is clicked, crop the canvas to the selected region and commit the result to the image state via the existing useImageCanvas hook.

In the Image Editor Text tab (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/TextOverlay.tsx and frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor.tsx), allow users to place text freely anywhere on the image by clicking or dragging on the canvas. After configuring text content, font, color, size, shadow, and outline in the text panel, the user should be able to click anywhere on the canvas preview to position the text at that exact location. Render the text layer at the clicked coordinates. The text layer must be draggable after placement so the user can reposition it freely by clicking and dragging the text on the canvas. The text layer must also support rotation by dragging a rotation handle. All text layers must be stored in and rendered by the existing useImageCanvas hook's text layer state.

Add a 'Video Downloader' tab to the existing TabNavigation component (frontend/src/components/TabNavigation.tsx) alongside the five existing tabs (Image Editor, PDF Converter, Image Converter, Passport Photo, BG Remover). Update App.tsx to conditionally render the new VideoDownloader module component when this tab is active. Maintain existing tab styling, scroll behavior, and smooth switching animations.

Create a new frontend module at frontend/src/modules/VideoDownloader.tsx. The module must provide: (1) A URL input field where users paste a YouTube or Facebook video link. (2) A 'Download' button that, when clicked, calls the YouTube Data API v3 using the API key AIzaSyDlj5yvdIpE6OYJnIw_oJg5Cz5_kRJ33YQ to fetch video metadata (title, thumbnail, available formats/qualities) for YouTube links. For Facebook links, display the pasted URL and attempt to resolve downloadable stream info via a client-side fetch to publicly accessible embed endpoints. (3) Display the video thumbnail and title once metadata is fetched. (4) Show available quality options (e.g., 360p, 720p, 1080p) when available and let the user select one. (5) Trigger a browser download of the video file via an anchor tag with the download attribute, or open the direct stream URL in a new tab if direct download is blocked by CORS. (6) Show a loading spinner while fetching metadata and a clear error message if the link is invalid, unsupported, or the API returns an error.

Apply the existing dark-first glassmorphism design system to the VideoDownloader module. Use glass-card surfaces for the input/result panels, tool-btn classes for action buttons, teal/cyan accent colors for the Download button and active states, and deep charcoal/slate backgrounds consistent with frontend/src/index.css utility classes. Ensure the module is fully mobile-responsive with the URL input and result area stacking vertically on small screens.

In the VideoDownloader module (frontend/src/modules/VideoDownloader.tsx), fix all errors related to the download functionality. The YouTube Data API v3 (API key: AIzaSyDlj5yvdIpE6OYJnIw_oJg5Cz5_kRJ33YQ) only returns metadata (title, thumbnail, video ID) and does not provide direct downloadable video stream URLs. Update the download flow so that: (1) For YouTube links, after fetching metadata via the YouTube Data API, the 'Download' button opens the best available direct stream URL in a new browser tab (e.g., a YouTube watch URL or embed URL) since direct binary download is blocked by CORS; display a clear user-facing message explaining the video will open in a new tab for saving. (2) For Facebook links, attempt a client-side fetch to the Facebook oEmbed endpoint (https://www.facebook.com/plugins/video/oembed.json?url=<encoded_url>) to retrieve the video URL, then open the resolved URL in a new tab; if the fetch fails with a CORS or network error, display a clear error message stating the link could not be resolved and suggest opening the link manually. (3) Ensure the download anchor tag or new-tab open does not throw uncaught JavaScript errors (e.g., null reference on missing URL, unhandled promise rejections). (4) If no quality option is selected, default to the first available quality before attempting the download. (5) Show a visible, user-friendly error message (styled consistently with the existing glassmorphism design) whenever the download action fails for any reason.

In the Image Converter module (frontend/src/modules/ImageConverter.tsx and its sub-panels), display a persistent preview of the currently selected/loaded image on the left side of the workspace across all four tabs (Upload, Settings, Preview, Export). The preview panel should show the active image at all times once at least one image has been uploaded, and update when the user selects a different image from the batch. On mobile, the preview should stack above the tab content.

In the Passport Photo Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/usePassportPhoto.ts), ensure that all canvas rendering and export operations use the maximum available resolution. When exporting via Canvas.toDataURL, use a scale factor that renders at the full target DPI (300 DPI for standard passport sizes) without downscaling. The output PNG, PDF, and ZIP exports should all be at this high-quality resolution.

In the Passport Photo Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/ExportPanel.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter.tsx), add GIF as an export format option alongside the existing PNG and PDF options. When GIF is selected, export the A4 sheet or individual passport photo copies as a GIF image using a client-side approach (e.g., Canvas toDataURL with image/gif MIME type, or a CDN-loaded GIF encoder library). Include GIF in the format selector dropdown/radio group.

In the Passport Photo Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter.tsx), display a preview of the uploaded and processed passport photo at the top of the module workspace, above the size/copies controls and the A4 grid. The preview should show the enhanced/cropped passport photo as it will appear in each copy, updating in real-time when the user changes size presets or enhancements.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BrushToolPanel.tsx), update the brush/paint tool workflow so that: (1) The user activates the Paint tool button to enter paint mode and paints over the area of the image they want to remove (the selected area is highlighted with a semi-transparent red overlay as already implemented). (2) After painting the desired area, the user clicks a dedicated 'Erase' button (distinct from the existing erase-mode toggle) that processes the canvas and removes only the painted/selected pixels — making them transparent (PNG output) or replacing them with the chosen solid color (JPG output). (3) The 'Erase' button must be prominently visible in the BrushToolPanel or the main toolbar whenever there are painted brush strokes on the canvas (i.e., the mask is non-empty). (4) The existing 'Remove Background' flow in RemovalPanel.tsx must continue to work as before for auto-detect and full background removal. (5) The Erase button must be styled using the existing tool-btn and teal/cyan accent CSS classes consistent with the glassmorphism design system.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx), reorder the main workspace layout so that the Before/After Comparison panel (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BeforeAfterPreview.tsx) is displayed at the top of the workspace, and the Brush Mode / Paint areas to erase panel (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BrushToolPanel.tsx) is displayed below it at the bottom. All other panels (RemovalPanel, CropOverlay, ExportPanel) must remain functional and their relative order should be preserved beneath or alongside the brush tool panel as appropriate.

In the Video Downloader module (frontend/src/modules/VideoDownloader.tsx), replace the single URL input field with two separate, clearly labeled URL input fields: one dedicated to Facebook video links (labeled 'Facebook Video URL') and one dedicated to YouTube video links (labeled 'YouTube Video URL'). Each input field must have its own independent 'Download' button. The Facebook input triggers the existing Facebook oEmbed fetch-and-open flow. The YouTube input triggers the existing YouTube Data API v3 metadata fetch (API key: AIzaSyDlj5yvdIpE6OYJnIw_oJg5Cz5_kRJ33YQ) and opens the resolved URL in a new tab. Each section must independently display its own loading spinner, error messages, video thumbnail, title, and quality selector (for YouTube). Both sections must be styled using the existing glassmorphism design system (glass-card surfaces, tool-btn classes, teal/cyan accent colors) and be fully mobile-responsive, stacking vertically on small screens.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx), ensure the Before/After Comparison panel (BeforeAfterPreview.tsx) is rendered at the top of the workspace and the Brush Mode / Paint areas to erase panel (BrushToolPanel.tsx) is rendered directly below it. All other panels (RemovalPanel, CropOverlay, ExportPanel) must remain functional and appear beneath the Brush Tool panel in their existing relative order.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BrushToolPanel.tsx), fix the canvas so that it does not shift, scroll, or reposition when the user paints or selects an area with the brush tool. The canvas container must have a fixed position and stable dimensions during all brush interaction events (mousedown, mousemove, mouseup, touchstart, touchmove, touchend). Prevent any layout reflow or scroll-jump triggered by brush strokes.

In the Image Converter module (frontend/src/modules/ImageConverter.tsx and its sub-panels), audit and fix all errors related to the persistent left-side image preview panel. Ensure the preview image loads and displays correctly when an image is uploaded, updates when the user switches between images in a batch, and does not show a broken or missing preview at any tab (Upload, Settings, Preview, Export). Handle null or undefined image sources gracefully.

In the Passport Photo Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/CopyGridPanel.tsx), audit and fix all errors related to the passport photo preview displayed at the top of the workspace and within the A4 copy grid. Ensure the processed/enhanced passport photo renders correctly after upload, updates in real-time when size presets or copy count change, and that the A4 canvas grid renders all copies without blank or broken images. Handle cases where the canvas or image data is not yet available.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BeforeAfterPreview.tsx), audit and fix all errors related to the before/after comparison preview images. Ensure the original image and the background-removed result both render correctly in the comparison slider. Handle cases where the result image is not yet available (show a placeholder or disabled state instead of a broken image). Fix any null reference errors when either image source is missing.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PageManager.tsx), audit and fix all errors related to PDF page thumbnail previews rendered via PDF.js. Ensure page thumbnails load and display correctly for uploaded PDF files, and do not show blank or broken thumbnails. Handle PDF.js load failures from CDN gracefully with a user-facing error message. Fix any null canvas or rendering context errors.

In the Passport Photo Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/TextCustomizationPanel.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/usePassportPhoto.ts), add a 'Text Background Color' control to the text overlay panel. This should include a color picker that lets the user choose a background color rendered behind the text on each passport photo copy, and a toggle or opacity slider to enable/disable the background. The selected background color must be applied when rendering the text overlay on the A4 canvas grid in CopyGridPanel.tsx.

In the Passport Photo Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/TextCustomizationPanel.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PassportPhotoConverter/usePassportPhoto.ts), add text shadow adjustment controls to the text overlay panel. Provide at minimum a shadow toggle (enable/disable), a shadow color picker, a shadow blur radius slider (range 0–20px), and horizontal/vertical shadow offset sliders (range -10 to 10px). Apply the configured shadow when rendering text on the A4 canvas grid in CopyGridPanel.tsx using the Canvas 2D context's shadowColor, shadowBlur, shadowOffsetX, and shadowOffsetY properties.

In the Image Converter module (frontend/src/modules/ImageConverter.tsx and its sub-panels), add both a 'Next' button and a 'Back' button to each tab in the tabbed interface (Upload, Settings, Preview, Export) to enable bidirectional linear navigation. On the Upload tab: show only a 'Next' button (no Back, as it is the first tab) that advances to the Settings tab; it must be disabled when no images have been uploaded. On the Settings tab: show a 'Back' button that returns to the Upload tab and a 'Next' button that advances to the Preview tab. On the Preview tab: show a 'Back' button that returns to the Settings tab and a 'Next' button that advances to the Export tab. On the Export tab: show a 'Back' button that returns to the Preview tab (no Next button, as it is the last tab); the existing export/download controls remain on this tab. Both buttons must appear prominently at the bottom of each tab panel, styled using the existing tool-btn and teal/cyan accent CSS classes consistent with the glassmorphism design system.

In the Image Editor Text Overlay panel (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/TextOverlay.tsx) and the useImageCanvas hook (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/useImageCanvas.ts), add a 'Text Background Color' control. This must include a color picker that lets the user choose a background color rendered as a filled rectangle behind the text on the canvas, and a toggle (enable/disable) to turn the background on or off. The selected background color and toggle state must be stored in each text layer's data model within useImageCanvas and applied when rendering text layers on the canvas.

In the Image Editor Text Overlay panel (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/TextOverlay.tsx) and the useImageCanvas hook (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/useImageCanvas.ts), add text shadow adjustment controls. Provide: a shadow toggle (enable/disable), a shadow color picker, a shadow blur radius slider (range 0–20px), and horizontal/vertical shadow offset sliders (range -10 to 10px). Apply the configured shadow when rendering text layers on the canvas using the Canvas 2D context's shadowColor, shadowBlur, shadowOffsetX, and shadowOffsetY properties. All shadow properties must be stored per text layer in useImageCanvas.

Remove the 'Video Downloader' tab from the TabNavigation component (frontend/src/components/TabNavigation.tsx) so that only the five original tabs remain: Image Editor, PDF Converter, Image Converter, Passport Photo, and BG Remover. Update App.tsx to remove the conditional render of the VideoDownloader module and any related state references to the 'video-downloader' tab. The VideoDownloader.tsx module file may remain in the codebase but must not be reachable from the UI.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/useBackgroundRemover.ts), audit and fix all errors that prevent the uploaded image from appearing in the canvas preview after the user selects a file via the 'Browse Photo' button. Ensure the image is correctly read via FileReader (or equivalent), the resulting data URL is stored in state, and the canvas is drawn with the loaded image immediately after the image onload event fires. Fix any null canvas reference, missing canvas context, incorrect canvas dimensions, or race condition between image load and canvas draw that could cause a blank or missing preview.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/useBackgroundRemover.ts), ensure the image load handler correctly sets both the source image and triggers a canvas redraw synchronously after the HTMLImageElement's onload callback fires. Verify the canvas ref is attached to the DOM before attempting to get the 2D rendering context. If the canvas ref is not yet available at load time, defer the draw until the ref is populated (e.g., via a useEffect dependency on the canvas ref and the loaded image data URL).

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFFileList.tsx), update the Files tab to support adding both PDF files and image files (JPG, PNG, JPEG, WEBP) in the same file list. The upload zone and 'Add Files' button must accept both PDF and image MIME types. Each item in the file list must display its filename, file type badge (PDF or Image), and an individual 'Remove' button to delete it from the list. The file list state in usePDFOperations.ts must track both PDF entries and image entries as separate typed items within a unified list.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/CreatePDFFromImages.tsx), implement a 'Convert & Download' workflow on the Create tab. When the user clicks the 'Convert' or 'Create PDF' button, merge all files currently in the unified file list (PDFs and images) into a single output PDF using pdf-lib loaded from CDN: embed each image file as a full-page image in the PDF, and append all pages from each PDF file in their listed order. After conversion completes, display a 'Download PDF' button that triggers a browser download of the resulting PDF file. Show a loading/progress indicator while the conversion is in progress and a success message when the download is ready.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx), remove the 'Pages' tab entirely from the tab bar and all associated tab navigation logic. The five-tab interface should become a four-tab interface: Files, Merge/Split, Convert, and Create. Remove any references to the Pages tab in the tab state, tab labels, and Next button flow. The PageManager component may remain in the codebase but must not be rendered from the main tab UI.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/MergeSplitTools.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts), redesign the Merge/Split tab so that when the user imports a PDF file, all pages of that PDF are rendered as thumbnail previews directly within the Merge/Split tab using PDF.js (loaded from CDN). Display each page as a numbered thumbnail in a scrollable grid. Render page thumbnails using the same PDF.js approach used in the existing PageManager component.

Within the redesigned Merge/Split tab (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/MergeSplitTools.tsx), add the ability to remove individual pages from the imported PDF. Each page thumbnail must have a visible 'Remove' button (e.g., an ✕ icon overlay). Clicking it marks that page for removal from the final output. Removed pages should be visually indicated (e.g., dimmed or crossed out) and excluded when the PDF is merged/exported.

Within the redesigned Merge/Split tab (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/MergeSplitTools.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts), add the ability to insert additional image files (JPG, PNG, JPEG, WEBP) and additional PDF files into the page sequence alongside the already-loaded PDF pages. Provide an 'Add Image' button and an 'Add PDF' button (or a single 'Add Files' button accepting both types) that opens a file picker. Each added image is appended as a new full-page entry in the page sequence. Each added PDF's pages are appended in order to the page sequence. All entries (original PDF pages, added images, added PDFs) must be displayed together in the unified scrollable thumbnail grid in their current order.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/MergeSplitTools.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts), add a 'Create PDF' or 'Merge & Download' action button at the bottom of the Merge/Split tab. When clicked, generate a merged PDF from the current page sequence (all non-removed entries: original PDF pages rendered via PDF.js, added images embedded as full-page images, added PDF pages) using pdf-lib loaded from CDN. After generation, trigger a browser download of the resulting PDF file. Show a loading/progress indicator during processing and a success message when the download is ready.

Add a 'PDF Scanner' tab to the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx) alongside the existing four tabs (Files, Merge/Split, Convert, Create). The tab must be labeled 'PDF Scanner' and rendered in the tab bar with the same styling as the other tabs. Update the tab state and navigation logic to include this new tab. Maintain all existing tab functionality and Next/Back button flows for the other tabs.

Create a new frontend component at frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx. This component must provide a 'Live Scanner' button that, when clicked, requests camera access via the browser MediaDevices API (navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia with video constraints) and opens a live camera viewfinder (a <video> element) within the panel. Display a clear permission-denied error message if the user denies camera access. Show a loading indicator while the camera stream is initializing.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), add a 'Capture' button that appears while the live camera viewfinder is active. When clicked, it captures the current video frame onto a hidden <canvas> element and produces a data URL of the captured image. The captured image is then displayed as a still preview alongside the live viewfinder, ready for crop and adjustment steps. The user must be able to capture multiple documents one by one; each captured frame is appended to a list of scanned page images.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), implement document crop selection for each captured image. Provide two crop modes: (1) Auto crop — automatically detect the document edges using a Canvas-based edge/contour detection approach (e.g., analysing pixel contrast or optionally loading OpenCV.js from CDN) and apply a perspective crop to the detected quadrilateral; (2) Manual crop — display an interactive crop overlay on the captured image with draggable corner handles, allowing the user to define the exact crop region. A toggle or radio control must let the user switch between Auto and Manual mode. Provide an 'Apply Crop' button to confirm the crop and a 'Reset' button to revert to the uncropped capture.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), provide per-page image adjustment controls for each scanned and cropped page. The controls must include: a Brightness slider (range -100 to +100), a Contrast slider (range -100 to +100), and a 'Darken Text' toggle that, when enabled, applies a threshold or levels filter via Canvas to increase text legibility (boosting dark pixels and lightening background). Apply all adjustments in real time on the Canvas preview of the currently selected scanned page. Adjustments must be stored per page so each scanned document can have independent settings.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), implement a scanned pages list panel that displays thumbnails of all captured and processed pages in order. Each thumbnail must show its page number and a remove button (✕ icon) to delete that page from the list. The user must be able to scan additional pages by returning to the camera viewfinder and capturing more frames, appending them to the existing list.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), implement a 'Convert to PDF' button that, when clicked, takes all scanned and processed page canvases (with their individual crop and adjustment settings applied) and generates a single PDF document using pdf-lib loaded from CDN (following the same CDN-load pattern used in usePDFOperations.ts). Each scanned page image is embedded as a full-page entry in the output PDF. Show a loading/progress indicator during generation. After generation completes, display a 'Download PDF' button that triggers a browser file download of the resulting PDF.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), add a filename input field and a 'Rename' control that allows the user to specify a custom filename for the exported PDF before downloading. The input must be pre-filled with a default name (e.g., 'scanned-document.pdf'). When the user types a new name and triggers the download, the downloaded file must use the specified filename. Validate that the filename is non-empty and append '.pdf' automatically if the user omits the extension.

Apply the existing dark-first glassmorphism design system to the PDF Scanner tab and the PDFScanner component. Use glass-card surfaces for the camera viewfinder panel, scanned pages list, adjustment controls panel, and export controls. Use tool-btn CSS classes for action buttons (Live Scanner, Capture, Apply Crop, Convert to PDF, Download PDF). Use teal/cyan accent colors for active states and primary action buttons. Ensure the layout is fully mobile-responsive, stacking the camera viewfinder, scanned pages list, and adjustment/export controls vertically on small screens.

In the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), fix the camera permission request flow so that when the user clicks the 'Live Scanner' button, the browser's native camera permission dialog is correctly triggered via navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia. If the user has not yet granted permission, the browser prompt must appear. While the permission prompt is pending or the stream is initializing, display a visible inline message reading 'Please allow camera access' to guide the user. If the user denies permission (NotAllowedError or NotFoundError), dismiss the loading state and display a clear, persistent error message: 'Camera access was denied. Please allow camera access in your browser settings and try again.' If permission is granted and the stream initializes successfully, hide the message and display the live camera viewfinder as before.

In the PDF Converter module (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx), reorder the tab bar so that the 'PDF Scanner' tab appears as the first tab, followed by the remaining tabs in their existing order: Files, Merge/Split, Convert, and Create. Update the tab state initialization so that 'PDF Scanner' is the default active tab when the PDF Converter module is first opened. Ensure all existing tab navigation logic (Next/Back button flows) remains functional with the new tab order.

In the PDF Converter module Files tab (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFFileList.tsx), add a prominent 'Create PDF' button below the file list. When clicked, it must trigger the same convert-and-download workflow already implemented in the Convert tab (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/ConvertDownload.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts): merge all files currently in the unified file list (PDFs and images) into a single output PDF using pdf-lib loaded from CDN, then trigger a browser download of the resulting PDF. Show a loading/progress indicator during processing and a success message when the download is ready. The button must be disabled when the file list is empty. Style the button using the existing tool-btn and teal/cyan accent CSS classes consistent with the glassmorphism design system.

In the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), add a 'Choose from Files' button alongside the existing 'Live Scanner' button as an alternative input method. When clicked, this button opens a native file picker that accepts image files (JPG, PNG, JPEG, WEBP) and PDF files. Selected files are imported and appended to the scanned pages list exactly as if they had been captured by the camera: image files are added directly as scanned page entries, and PDF files have their pages rendered as individual scanned page entries using PDF.js (loaded from CDN). The imported pages must appear in the scanned pages thumbnail list and participate in all subsequent crop, adjustment, and 'Convert to PDF' export operations. Style the 'Choose from Files' button using the existing tool-btn CSS class and the glassmorphism design system consistent with the rest of the PDF Scanner panel. On mobile, the button label should read 'Choose from Files (Google Files / Scanner)' to guide users toward their device file manager or scanner app.

In the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), remove the 'Live Scanner' camera button entirely. Replace it with only a 'Choose from Files' button (on mobile, label it 'Choose from Files (Google Files / Scanner)'). When clicked, open a native file picker accepting image files (JPG, PNG, JPEG, WEBP) and PDF files. Selected image files are added directly as scanned page entries. Selected PDF files have their pages rendered as individual scanned page entries using PDF.js loaded from CDN. All imported pages must appear in the scanned pages thumbnail list and participate in all subsequent crop, adjustment, and 'Convert to PDF' export operations. Remove all camera/getUserMedia/video-element code from the component.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), after a user imports image files via the 'Choose from Files' button, automatically detect document/page edges for each imported image using a Canvas-based edge detection algorithm (pixel contrast analysis or equivalent). Apply an automatic perspective crop to the detected document quadrilateral without requiring manual user interaction. Display the auto-cropped result as the default preview for each scanned page. Provide a 'Manual Crop' fallback mode with draggable corner handles so the user can override the automatic crop if needed. Provide an 'Apply Crop' button to confirm and a 'Reset' button to revert to the uncropped original.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), provide per-page image adjustment controls for each scanned/imported page: a Brightness slider (range -100 to +100), a Contrast slider (range -100 to +100), and a 'Darken Text' toggle that applies a threshold/levels filter via Canvas to boost dark pixels and lighten the background for improved text legibility. Apply all adjustments in real time on the Canvas preview of the currently selected page. Store adjustments per page so each page can have independent settings.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), ensure the scanned pages list panel displays thumbnails of all imported and processed pages in order. Each thumbnail must show its page number and a remove (✕) button to delete that page from the list. The user must be able to import additional pages at any time using the 'Choose from Files' button, appending them to the existing list.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), implement a 'Convert to PDF' button that takes all scanned and processed pages (with individual crop and adjustment settings applied) and generates a single PDF document using pdf-lib loaded from CDN (following the same CDN-load pattern used in usePDFOperations.ts). Each page image is embedded as a full-page entry in the output PDF. Show a loading/progress indicator during generation. After generation completes, display a 'Download PDF' button that triggers a browser file download of the resulting PDF.

Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), add a filename input field that allows the user to specify a custom filename for the exported PDF before downloading. Pre-fill the input with a default name (e.g., 'scanned-document.pdf'). When the download is triggered, use the specified filename. Validate that the filename is non-empty; if the user omits the '.pdf' extension, append it automatically.

Apply the existing dark-first glassmorphism design system to all updated and new UI elements within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx). Use glass-card surfaces for the file import panel, scanned pages list, adjustment controls panel, and export controls. Use tool-btn CSS classes for all action buttons ('Choose from Files', 'Apply Crop', 'Reset', 'Convert to PDF', 'Download PDF'). Use teal/cyan accent colors for active states and primary action buttons. Ensure the layout is fully mobile-responsive, stacking the file import area, scanned pages list, and adjustment/export controls vertically on small screens.

In the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), update the 'Choose from Files' file input element so that it also accepts camera capture as an input source on mobile and desktop devices. Set the HTML file input's 'capture' attribute to 'environment' (rear camera preferred) and ensure the 'accept' attribute includes image MIME types (image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp) alongside PDF. On mobile devices, this causes the native OS file picker to present 'Camera' as a selectable option alongside the file browser; when the user selects 'Camera', the device's native camera app opens in document/scan mode. Any image captured via the camera must be appended to the scanned pages list exactly as images selected from the file system are — added directly as scanned page entries and processed through the existing auto edge-detection, crop, adjustment, and 'Convert to PDF' export workflow. Do not add a separate button; the camera option must be accessible through the existing 'Choose from Files' button's file picker dialog.

In the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), add a dedicated 'Camera' button alongside the existing 'Choose from Files' button. When the user clicks the 'Camera' button, it must trigger the device's native camera directly — on mobile this opens the system camera app in document/scan mode, and on desktop it requests camera access via the browser. Implement this by creating a separate hidden file input element with accept='image/*' and capture='environment' attributes (without PDF in the accept list), so that tapping it on mobile devices immediately launches the system camera rather than showing a file picker. The captured image is appended to the scanned pages list as a new scanned page entry and processed through the existing auto edge-detection, crop, adjustment, and 'Convert to PDF' export workflow, exactly as images imported via 'Choose from Files' are handled.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BrushToolPanel.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/useBackgroundRemover.ts), fix and improve the brush painting interaction so that strokes are rendered smoothly on the canvas without gaps, jitter, or lag. Use continuous interpolation between pointer events (connect consecutive mouse/touch points with line segments on the mask canvas) so that fast brush movements produce a solid, unbroken painted path. The semi-transparent red overlay must update in real time as the user paints without flickering or partial redraws.

In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BrushToolPanel.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/useBackgroundRemover.ts), replace the existing fixed brush-size preset buttons (small/medium/large) with a continuous brush thickness slider (range: 4px to 100px) that allows the user to set any brush size within that range. Display the current brush size value numerically next to the slider. The selected brush size must be immediately applied to subsequent brush strokes on the canvas. The existing preset size buttons may be removed or replaced entirely by this slider.

In the PDF Converter Files tab (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFFileList.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter.tsx), add a file selection mechanism so users can select individual files one by one via checkboxes on each file list item, or select all files at once via a 'Select All' checkbox/button. The existing 'Create PDF' button (already present below the file list) must operate on all currently selected files. If no files are explicitly selected, it should default to all files in the list. The selected-state must be visually indicated (e.g., highlighted row or checked checkbox). Style selection controls using the existing glassmorphism design system (tool-btn, teal/cyan accent classes).

In the PDF Converter Merge/Split tab (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/MergeSplitTools.tsx and frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/usePDFOperations.ts), add a file/page selection mechanism so users can select individual page entries one by one via checkboxes on each thumbnail, or select all entries via a 'Select All' control. Additionally, implement drag-to-reorder for all entries in the unified page sequence thumbnail grid: each thumbnail must be draggable and droppable onto another position so users can change the serial order (shift files/pages relative to one another) before generating the PDF. The existing 'Merge & Download' / 'Create PDF' action button at the bottom of the tab must generate the merged PDF using the current (reordered, non-removed) page sequence and trigger a browser file download. Style all new controls using the existing glassmorphism design system.

Remove all camera/getUserMedia/MediaDevices API access and any related UI controls (camera buttons, video elements, capture buttons) from every module EXCEPT the PDF Scanner tab component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx). Specifically, audit and remove camera access from: the Image Editor module (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/ImageUploadPanel.tsx — remove the 'Capture via Camera' option), the PDF Converter CreatePDFFromImages component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/CreatePDFFromImages.tsx — remove any camera capture button), and any other module or component outside of PDFScanner.tsx that calls navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia or renders a camera input. The PDF Scanner component must retain its existing camera/file-based capture functionality untouched.

Create a new standalone React application (separate from the existing conversion tool) at the root level with a tabbed navigation layout containing four tabs: 'MP3 Cutter', 'Video Cutter', 'Video Downloader', and 'IP Camera'. Include a dark mode / light mode toggle in the header. The layout must be fully mobile-responsive with smooth tab-switching animations.

Implement the MP3 Cutter module. Provide a file upload button that accepts MP3, AAC, WAV, and MP4 files. After upload, load the audio using the Web Audio API and render a waveform visualization on a Canvas element. Display the audio duration. Allow the user to drag two range handles (start and end markers) on the waveform to select a segment of the audio track. Show the selected start time and end time numerically. Provide a 'Cut & Download' button that, when clicked, extracts the selected audio segment client-side (using AudioBuffer slicing via the Web Audio API and encoding to MP3 using a CDN-loaded lamejs or similar encoder library), then triggers a browser download of the trimmed audio as an MP3 file.

Implement the Video Cutter module. Provide a file upload button that accepts all common video formats (MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, MKV). After upload, display the video in an HTML5 <video> element for playback preview. Render a timeline scrubber with two draggable handles (start and end trim points) so the user can define the clip region. Show the selected start and end times numerically. Provide a 'Cut & Download' button that uses the MediaRecorder API or ffmpeg.wasm (loaded lazily from CDN) to extract and encode the selected video segment, then triggers a browser download of the trimmed video file. Show a loading/progress indicator during processing.

Implement the Video Downloader module. Provide a URL input field where users paste a YouTube video link. Provide a 'Download' button that, when clicked, calls the YouTube Data API v3 using the API key AIzaSyDlj5yvdIpE6OYJnIw_oJg5Cz5_kRJ33YQ to fetch video metadata (title, thumbnail). Display the video thumbnail and title. Since direct binary download of YouTube videos is blocked by CORS, the 'Download' button must open the YouTube watch URL in a new browser tab and display a clear user-facing message: 'The video will open in a new tab — use your browser or a download extension to save it.' Show a loading spinner while fetching metadata and a user-friendly error message if the URL is invalid or the API returns an error.

Implement the IP Camera module. Provide an 'IP Cam' button that, when clicked, requests camera access via navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia (video: true) and streams the live video feed into an HTML5 <video> element displayed within the panel. Simultaneously, detect the device's local WiFi IP address by fetching from a public STUN-based WebRTC ICE candidate gathering approach (using RTCPeerConnection with a STUN server to extract the local IP from ICE candidates). Display the detected local IP address prominently (e.g., 'Access camera at: http://<local-ip>:PORT') along with a note that any browser on the same WiFi network can use that address to view the stream. Display a clear error message if camera access is denied. Show a 'Stop Camera' button to stop the stream and release the camera.

Apply a cohesive dark-first visual design system to the entire Multimedia App. Use deep charcoal/slate backgrounds, vibrant teal/cyan accent colors for buttons and active states, crisp white typography, subtle glassmorphism card surfaces (backdrop-filter blur with semi-transparent backgrounds), and smooth micro-interaction animations (button hover states, tab transitions, progress indicators). Enforce this design system consistently across all four module tabs (MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, IP Camera). Reuse the glass-card and tool-btn CSS utility class patterns consistent with the existing app's index.css.

new_feature_requests.summary: Image Editor Module: crop, resize, rotate/flip, brightness/contrast/saturation, text editing with fonts/colors/shadow, filters, multi-format save
PDF Converter Module: merge/split PDFs, remove/rearrange pages, create PDF, convert images to PDF, clear picture enhancement, compress PDF
Image Converter Module: format conversion (JPG/PNG/WEBP/PDF), resize by dimensions or percentage, quality adjustment, filters, batch conversion, before/after preview
UI: dark/light mode toggle, tabbed navigation (Image Editor, PDF Converter, Image Converter), mobile-responsive, smooth animations

The backend actor should expose a minimal health-check or metadata endpoint (e.g., getAppInfo returning app name and version) so the frontend can confirm the canister is reachable. No image or PDF data should be stored in the backend; all processing must remain client-side.

Passport Size Photo Converter: upload JPG/PNG/JPEG, auto-convert to standard passport size (35x45mm or customizable), HD enhancement, brightness/contrast/sharpness auto-adjust, number of copies option, A4 sheet grid layout, add text with font/size/color/bold/italic/alignment, download as PNG/PDF, print button

Image Background Remover: upload image, crop with adjustable box (resize/rotate/zoom), brush selection tool with adjustable size and undo, remove selected background (transparent PNG or custom color), before/after preview, download PNG/JPG, optional AI auto-detect, background color picker, add new background image, zoom in/out, reset button
Image Editor: add Download button after editing to export/download the edited image
PDF Converter: add Next button flow — Files tab → Next → Merge/Split tab → Next → Pages tab → Next → Create tab → download after process completes
Image Converter: add Next button navigation across all tabs/steps in the converter workflow
Add ZIP download option to all applicable modules: Image Editor, Passport Photo Converter, Background Remover, and PDF Converter (batch export as zip)
Image Editor: add a visible Crop button in the UI for cropping the image
Convert/package the web app as an APK (Android app)
Image Editor Crop tab: clicking Crop button lets user select/define crop area on the image interactively; Text tab: user can add text anywhere on the image by clicking/dragging a text overlay
Image Converter: show loaded image preview on the left side across all tabs in the Image Converter module
Passport Photo Converter: ensure high-quality photo resolution output, add GIF as an export format option, and show photo preview at the top of the module
Background Remover: user paints/selects an area using the brush/paint tool, then clicks an 'Erase' button to remove the background from the selected area of the photo
Background Remover: reorder layout so Before/After Comparison window is at the top and Brush Mode (Paint areas to erase) panel is at the bottom

In the Image Editor module (frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor.tsx and frontend/src/modules/ImageEditor/useImageCanvas.ts), audit and fix all errors related to the canvas preview image display. Ensure the image renders correctly after upload, after applying any edits (brightness, contrast, saturation, rotate, flip, crop, filters, text overlays), and does not display a blank, broken, or stale canvas at any point. Handle edge cases such as image load failures, null canvas references, and out-of-order state updates.
Passport Photo Converter: in the Text Overlay panel, add text background color picker and text shadow adjustment controls
Image Converter: add both Next and Back buttons for tab navigation — Next advances to the next tab, Back returns to the previous tab
Image Editor Text Overlay: add text background color picker and text shadow adjustment controls (shadow toggle, color, blur radius, offset sliders)
Background Remover: fix image not showing in preview after user uploads a picture
PDF Converter: allow users to select PDF files and also add/remove individual images and PDFs, then convert/merge them into a final PDF file and download it
PDF Converter: remove the Pages tab; in Merge/Split tab, user imports a PDF and all pages are shown/explored; allow removing individual pages and adding images/PDFs in the Merge/Split tab

PDF Converter: add 'PDF Scanner' tab — user clicks Live Scanner button to open camera, scans documents one by one, auto/manual crop selection, brightness/contrast/darken-text adjustments per scan, then converts all scanned images into a single PDF with save and rename options

PDF Scanner: fix camera permission request flow — when user clicks Live Scanner, properly request camera access and show a clear 'Please allow camera access' prompt/message guiding the user to grant permission
PDF Converter: move the PDF Scanner tab to be the first tab in the tab bar
PDF Converter Files tab: add a 'Create PDF' button to convert/merge listed files into a PDF directly from the Files tab
PDF Scanner: add option to use device file picker (Google Files/scanner app) to import scanned documents instead of live camera, as an alternative input method

PDF Scanner: remove the Live Scanner (camera) button entirely; replace with 'Choose from Files' button only; selected images auto-detect page/document edges and apply automatic crop; allow brightness/contrast adjustment and darken text toggle per page; scan multiple pages one by one; convert all to PDF with save and rename options

PDF Scanner: when user clicks 'Choose from Files' button, allow selecting camera as input source (system/mobile camera) so clicking camera option opens the device camera for scanning a document directly

Background Remover Brush Mode: ensure brush paints smoothly over the canvas for area selection, brush thickness (size) adjustment works correctly, and the selected area is clearly highlighted for erase
PDF Converter Files tab: allow selecting files one by one or all at once, then convert and merge them into a PDF using a 'Convert PDF' button
PDF Converter Merge/Split tab: allow adding files one by one or all at once, reorder/shift files by dragging (change serial order), and generate/download the merged PDF via a Download button
Remove camera/getUserMedia access from all modules except the PDF Scanner tab — only the PDF Scanner is allowed to request camera permission
New Multimedia App: MP3 Cutter — import MP3/AAC/WAV/MP4 files, select/cut audio track, download trimmed MP3
New Multimedia App: Video Cutter — import any video file, crop/trim video with cut button, download result
New Multimedia App: Video Downloader — paste YouTube link, one-click download button to download the video
New Multimedia App: IP Camera — start video capture via IP Cam button, display the device's local WiFi IP address so any browser on the same network can access the camera stream
